evals.prompt: Run a lite review of seven Markdown files. The diff changes 500 lines and only corrects user-guide wording and links.
evals.expected_output: Select Docs Tiny, use zero child agents, review inline, and produce a self-describing .lite.md report.
expectations: The profile is Docs Tiny because the change is documentation-only with no runtime effect; documentation size does not trigger child agents.
No Build Validator, Requirement Validator, or named specialist is dispatched.
The report records code-review-lite v2.1.2, copies known launch model and effort exactly into Main Runtime, records Branch Work Item Gate status, and explains skipped actors.

evals.prompt: Quick-review a one-file 42-line null-check fix in a private helper. It changes no API, shared behavior, state, config, dependency, auth, schema, or async lifecycle.
evals.expected_output: Select Code Tiny, run one Build Validator for the repository, and have the main agent review code without other child reviewers.
expectations: The profile is Code Tiny only after every Tiny risk exclusion is checked.
Exactly one dedicated Build Validator per repository is triggered with the active tool's fast runtime.

Branch Work Item Gate uses the Build Validator runtime, accepts branch IDs with optional text, treats prefix mismatch as WARN, then Requirement Validator and named specialists are skipped while the main agent performs the code review.

evals.prompt: Lite-review six changed files implementing cancellation and disposal for a background worker. No API, auth, schema, dependency, state, or config behavior changes.
evals.expected_output: Select Lite, run build and requirement validation, then one Performance Reviewer.
expectations: The profile is Lite because the change exceeds Tiny limits and triggers only Performance Reviewer.
One dedicated Build Validator per repository uses fast runtime and one dedicated Requirement Validator uses deep runtime.
Exactly one generic code-reviewer is triggered as Performance Reviewer with standard runtime after Branch Work Item Gate passes.

evals.prompt: Review an OAuth endpoint change that also modifies a public response schema and package lockfile.
evals.expected_output: Announce Security, Philosophy, and Standard triggers and route to code-review-pro without running the Lite pipeline.
expectations: Tiny is rejected regardless of file or line count because auth, public schema, and dependency risks are present.
More than one named specialist is identified.
The skill announces escalation and routes to code-review-pro instead of dispatching Lite agents or writing a Lite report.

non_goals: Replace code-review-pro for changes requiring multiple specialist families.
Publish review comments or modify Azure DevOps work items.
Sync or install the skill into deployed tool directories.
